none: Motorcycle noise emission regulations : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed new noise emission regulations for motorcycles to address the problem of excessive noise from both street and off-road motorcycles. The regulations aim to reduce noise pollution by setting stricter sound limits for new motorcycles and replacement exhaust systems. The proposal includes a phased approach to lower noise levels, with specific decibel limits set for different types of motorcycles. These changes are expected to reduce noise impact significantly, although they may increase motorcycle prices and affect performance. The regulations are designed to create a quieter environment, benefiting urban and suburban communities as well as natural areas.
Motorcycle noise emission regulations : environmental impact statement.

The U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) proposed new noise emission regulations for motorcycles to address the problem of excessive noise from both street and off-road motorcycles. The regulations aim to reduce noise pollution by setting stricter sound limits for new motorcycles and replacement exhaust systems. The proposal includes a phased approach to lower noise levels, with specific decibel limits set for different types of motorcycles. These changes are expected to reduce noise impact significantly, although they may increase motorcycle prices and affect performance. The regulations are designed to create a quieter environment, benefiting urban and suburban communities as well as natural areas.
Office of Noise Abatement and Control

The proposal for motorcycle noise emission regulations was initiated by the EPA in response to growing concerns about noise pollution affecting quality of life in urban and suburban areas. There was some public pushback from motorcycle enthusiasts and manufacturers worried about the potential impact on performance and costs. Ultimately, the regulations aimed to strike a balance between reducing noise pollution and maintaining the interests of the motorcycle community.
"The noise impact of new motorcycles, and especially of exhaust-modified motorcycles, constitutes a major noise problem." — Health and Welfare Impact of Motorcycle Operations

"Reducing street motorcycle sound emissions to the 78 dB(A) level will bring motorcycles closer to parity with sound levels of current automobiles and other vehicles operated in this environment." — Discussion of the Proposed Regulation
